code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping predicates to clause fragments (subject omitted)
    clause_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "second runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "third runway surface is {object}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "fourth runway surface is {object}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "fifth runway surface is {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway {object} feet long",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway {object} feet long",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number {object}",
        # add more as needed
    }
    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject
    subjects: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subjects.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subj, tlist in subjects.items():
        clauses = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = clause_map.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
            else:
                # fallback generic clause
                clause = f"{t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {t.object}"
            clauses.append(clause)
        # Build sentence for this subject
        if not clauses:
            continue
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentence = f"{subj} {clauses[0]}."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine multiple subject sentences into one paragraph (still a single string)
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable sentence."""
      if not triples:
          return ""
      # Simple template mapping for common predicates
      templates = {
          "cityServed": "{subject} serves {object}",
          "location": "{subject} is located in {object}",
          "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} lies {object} meters above sea level",
          "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{subject} lies {object} feet above sea level",
          "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{subject} lies {object} metres above sea level",

          tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
          if tmpl:
              phrase = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=t.object)
          else:
              phrase = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"
          phrases.append(phrase)
